texto: I only had to drop acid once to know that Timothy Leary was right about questioning authority.Motorcycling is like life. There's nothing solid about it. Something not even the asphalt under your tires.Time on a motorcycle is unlike time spent anywhere else. There are moments lost in the landscape, seconds devoted solely to balance, and long stretches spent spiraling inward.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Barbara Schoichet: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “I only had to drop acid once to know that Timothy Leary was right about questioning authority.Mot…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, inspiracional, journey pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e inspiracao.
